nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Clifford was born about 1590, in Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England as the daughter of James de Clifford. She married John Cage in 1610, in London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1650, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Mary Clifford est née environ 1590, à Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, fille de James de Clifford. Elle a épousé John Cage en 1610, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1650, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Mary Clifford wurde um 1590 in Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England, als Tochter von James de Clifford geboren. Sie hat 1610 in London, England, United Kingdom John Cage geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1650 im Alter von 62 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Mary Clifford è nata il circa 1590 nel Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England come figlia di James de Clifford. Lei sposato John Cage nel 1610, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1650, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Cliffordは約1590年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン オン セバーンでJames de Cliffordの娘として生まれた。1610年にイギリスイギリスロンドンでJohn Cageと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1650年にイギリスイギリスで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Clifford(은)는 대략 1590년 영국 그루스스터실르 프램프턴  세버에서 James de Clifford의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1610년 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 John Cage(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1650년 영국 잉글랜드에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Mary Clifford родилась около 1590 г. в местности фрамптон он сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия. Её отца звали James de Clifford. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cage в 1610 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1650 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет.
